spec.reasoning: The instruction references specific column letters on both Sheet1 (H, I) and Sheet2 (D, H, I, J, K, L, M) and a specific cell (I8). Previous attempts failed because distractor column insertions shifted column letters, breaking these references. Therefore, I only apply lexical renames to columns whose names are not used as English words in the instruction. 'ID'→'Seq', 'Mon'→'Mth', 'Tab'→'Idx', 'x1'→'z1' are abbreviation-to-abbreviation renames. 'Track'→'Venue' and 'Top'→'Best' and 'Rank'→'Place' are short-word-to-short-word renames. I avoid renaming 'Meet', 'Race#', 'Date', 'Year', 'Day' as they appear in or closely relate to instruction text. No distractor columns or layout changes are applied to avoid shifting column positions.
